name: acpi.sys
description: Advanced Configuration and Power Interface driver. Enumerates devices and manages power at the firmware boundary.
security_note: A Boot-start Ring 0 driver; corruption causes an unbootable system and it is a target for firmware-level bootkits.

name: pci.sys
description: Enumerates and configures devices on the PCI bus so higher-level drivers can attach.
security_note: Loads extremely early. Anything tampering with bus enumeration operates below most defensive tooling.

name: Wdf01000.sys
description: The KMDF runtime that most modern hardware drivers are built on top of.
security_note: Foundational; a flaw here would affect a huge range of dependent drivers running in Ring 0.

name: storahci.sys
description: Miniport driver for AHCI storage controllers - required to read the boot disk.
security_note: Boot-critical storage path; bootkits and disk rootkits hook the storage stack to survive reinstalls.

name: disk.sys
description: Presents physical disks to the OS as block devices.
security_note: Rootkits may filter this driver to hide sectors or intercept raw disk I/O.

name: volmgr.sys
description: Manages volumes and partitions on top of the disk driver.
security_note: Sits in the storage filter stack where both EDR and malware can attach filters.

name: ntfs.sys
description: Implements the NTFS file system, including journaling and alternate data streams.
security_note: Minifilter drivers (EDR AND malware) attach here. Alternate Data Streams are a classic hiding spot (T1564.004).

name: cdrom.sys
description: Class driver for optical (CD/DVD) drives. Demand-loaded; stopped when no optical media is present.
security_note: Benign example of a stopped, demand-start driver - a useful baseline for what 'not loaded' looks like.

name: ndis.sys
description: The Network Driver Interface Specification library that NIC drivers plug into.
security_note: NDIS lightweight filters can inspect all traffic - used legitimately by EDR and abusively by kernel sniffers.

name: tcpip.sys
description: Implements the TCP/IP stack in the kernel.
security_note: A large remote attack surface; historic RCE bugs (e.g. CVE-2021-24086) make patch level here critical.

name: e1i65x64.sys
description: Third-party miniport driver for Intel Gigabit network adapters.
security_note: Third-party signed driver. A vulnerable version is exactly what BYOVD attacks abuse to reach Ring 0 (T1068).

name: dxgkrnl.sys
description: Kernel component that mediates GPU access for user-mode graphics.
security_note: A large kernel attack surface reachable from low-privilege processes; a frequent local privilege-escalation target.

name: nvlddmkm.sys
security_note: Third-party GPU drivers have a history of Ring 0 vulnerabilities used for LPE and BYOVD - keep them patched.

name: kbdclass.sys
description: Class driver that abstracts keyboard input for the OS.
security_note: Kernel keyloggers attach filter drivers to the keyboard stack to capture keystrokes below user mode.

name: mouclass.sys
description: Class driver that abstracts pointing-device input.
security_note: Same filter-stack exposure as the keyboard driver; unexpected filters here warrant investigation.

name: WdFilter.sys
description: The Microsoft Defender minifilter driver that inspects file operations in real time.
security_note: Attackers try to unload or blind this minifilter. Enable tamper protection and alert on filter-unload attempts.
